info.name: TripTactician API
info.description:
Full API test collection for TripTactician.

IMPORT ORDER:
1. Import this collection
2. Import TripTactician.postman_environment.json
3. Select the TripTactician environment before running any requests

TESTING FLOW:
1. Health Check
2. Auth / Register → saves token + userId automatically
3. Auth / Login → alternative, saves token + userId automatically
4. Trips / Create Trip → saves tripId automatically
5. Trips / Set My Preferences
6. Invites / Create Invite → saves inviteToken automatically
7. Invites / Get Invite (validate token)
8. Invites / Accept Invite (as partner — use partnerToken)
9. Trips / Trigger Generation
10. Trips / Get Generation Status (poll until active)
11. Activities / Add Activity → saves activityId automatically
12. Activities / Get All Activities
13. Activities / Update Activity
14. Activities / Reorder Activities
15. Activities / Remove Activity
16. Notifications / Get All
17. Trips / Delete Trip → cleanup

All authenticated requests use {{token}} from environment automatically.
placeRoutes and fillGaps return 501 (not implemented yet).

request.description: No auth required. Should return { status: 'ok' }. Run this first to confirm the server is up.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Register and Login both auto-save token + userId to the environment.

request.description: Create a new user. Run once — use Login for subsequent sessions to avoid 409 duplicate email.

exec: if (res.token) { pm.environment.set('token', res.token); console.log('✓ token saved'); }
if (res.user?.id) { pm.environment.set('userId', res.user.id); console.log('✓ userId saved:', res.user.id); }

request.description: Login with existing credentials. Saves token and userId to environment.

exec: if (res.token) { pm.environment.set('token', res.token); console.log('✓ token saved'); }
if (res.user?.id) { pm.environment.set('userId', res.user.id); console.log('✓ userId saved:', res.user.id); }

request.description: Verify the current token is valid and see the logged-in user.
Update name and/or preferredCurrency. Both fields optional.

Always returns 200 regardless of whether the email exists (security by design).

NOTE: Email sending is a TODO. Retrieve the reset token directly from MongoDB:
db.users.findOne({ email: 'alex@test.com' }, { resetPasswordToken: 1 })

Set a new password using the token from forgot-password.
Set resetPasswordToken manually in environment from MongoDB.

Verify email with token from registration.
Set verificationToken from MongoDB:
db.users.findOne({ email: 'alex@test.com' }, { verificationToken: 1 })

item.name: Trips
item.description:
Trip CRUD, preferences, and generation. All routes require auth.
/:id routes also require participantCheck.

request.description: Returns all trips where the current user is a participant.

Creates a trip with the current user as owner.
Automatically builds a day entry for each date between startDate and endDate.
Status defaults to 'solo'.
Saves tripId to environment automatically.
All fields optional. Changing startDate or endDate rebuilds the days array.
Tests the date-change → day rebuild path. Check that days.length in the response equals the number of days between the new dates.

Upserts preferences for the current user on this trip.

Valid pace: 'relaxed' | 'moderate' | 'packed'
Valid interests: 'food' | 'culture' | 'nature' | 'nightlife' | 'shopping' | 'adventure' | 'wellness'

If both participants have prefs and status is 'pending_partner', status auto-changes to 'generating'.
Returns 404 if preferences not set yet.

exec: if (pm.response.code === 404) console.log('Preferences not set yet — run Set My Preferences first');

request.description: Read-only. Returns 404 if no partner, or partner hasn't set preferences yet.

Fire-and-forget — responds immediately. Generation runs in the background.
Poll Get Generation Status until status === 'active'.

Requirements: both participants must have preferences set.
NOTE: generationService has TODO stubs — won't fully complete until placesService + llmService are wired.

Poll after triggering generation. Returns { status, generatedAt }.
Poll every 2-3 seconds until status === 'active'.

exec: if (res.status === 'active') console.log('✓ Generation complete at', res.generatedAt);

request.description: Cascade-deletes activities and preferences. Clears tripId and activityId from environment.

exec: console.log('✓ Trip deleted, tripId + activityId cleared');

item.name: Activities
item.description:
All routes under /api/trips/:id/activities/*
All require auth + participantCheck (you must be a trip participant).

request.description: Returns all activities for the trip sorted by dayNumber then order.

exec: const byDay = acts.reduce((acc, a) => { acc[a.dayNumber] = (acc[a.dayNumber] || 0) + 1; return acc; }, {});
// Save first activityId for update/remove tests
console.log('✓ activityId set to first result:', acts[0]._id);

request.description:
Manually add an activity to a day.

Required: dayNumber, placeId, name
Optional: everything else

source auto-set to 'manual'
addedBy auto-set to current user
conflict defaults to { flagged: false }

Saves activityId to environment.

Pass ALL activity IDs for this day in the desired order.
Server assigns order: 1, 2, 3... by array position.

To test properly: add 2-3 activities, then send all IDs in a different order.
Returns the full updated activity list for the day sorted by new order.

Returns 501 until generationService is wired up.
When implemented: LLM suggests 2-3 activities to fill empty time slots in the day.
Remove an activity by ID. Clears activityId from environment.

item.name: Invites
item.description:
Invite a partner to join a trip.

Flow:
1. Create Invite (as owner) → saves inviteToken
2. Get Invite (validates token — no auth needed)
3. Accept Invite (as partner B — requires partnerToken in environment)
4. Resend Invite (if expired)

request.description:
Creates invite doc and sends email (email requires RESEND_API_KEY — stubs silently without it).
Expires in 7 days. Any existing pending invite for this email+trip is expired first.
Saves inviteToken to environment.

No auth required — mimics what happens when Partner B clicks the email link.
Returns populated trip name, dates, and inviter name.
404 = not found, 410 = expired.

exec: console.log('Token not found — check inviteToken in environment');

request.description:
Run this as Partner B:
1. Register a second user (sam@test.com) via Auth / Register
2. Copy the returned token
3. Set it as partnerToken in the environment manually
4. Run this request

On success: Partner B added to trip.participants, invite status → 'accepted'.
Partner B can then set preferences using partnerToken.
Creates a fresh invite, expiring the old one. Saves new inviteToken to environment.
Returns up to 50 notifications, newest first. Saves first notification ID to environment.
Run Get Notifications first to set notificationId.

exec: console.log('Not found — run Get Notifications first');

item.name: Places (Not Implemented)
item.description: Both return 501 until placesService is connected. Included so you know when they start working.

item.name: Error cases
item.description: Requests that should fail. Use these to verify error handling is correct.

request.description: Valid ObjectId format but not a real trip. Tests participantCheck middleware.
'turbo' is not a valid pace value. Tests Zod validateBody middleware returns 400.
Missing dayNumber and placeId. Should return 400 or 500 depending on Mongoose validation.
Register with an already-used email. Should return 409 Conflict.
Trigger generation twice in a row. Second call should return 409 if trip is already in 'generating' status.
